current_run.description: Given an N-bit integer x, a secret N-bit integer s, and a secret N-bit integer k, determine if the majority of pairs of bits (x_i, x_{i+1} mod N) are such that their bitwise AND is equal to the corresponding bits in k. Formally, we want to find s such that for all i in [0, N-1], the function f(x) = 1 if sum_{i=0}^{N-1} ((x_i AND x_{i+1 mod N}) == k_i) > N/2, and 0 otherwise. This is a non-linear function of x and depends on two secret values s and k.
